title: lotus repens
intro: Lotus repens, also known as creeping lotus or Egyptian lotus, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the genus Nelumbo. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly in Ayurvedic and Unani practices, due to its medicinal properties. The rhizomes and seeds of lotus repens contain various bioactive compounds, including alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of lotus repens are often used to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions, and are also believed to have anti-aging and immune-boosting properties.
